compiled_state.title: As a developer, sprint pre-flight validates all engines referenced in the plan
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Pre-flight engine check at sprint start that scans the plan for recipe: tasks, validates each referenced engine is installed and meets the minimum version from the registry, and reports issues before any tasks execute.
**Why**: Early detection of missing or outdated engines prevents mid-sprint failures and gives the user a chance to install engines before task execution begins.
**Scope**:
- IN: Pre-flight scan of plan for recipe: tasks, engine presence + version validation against registry's minimum_version field, structured report of missing/outdated engines, populates missing-engines list for S5 fallback consumption
- OUT: Fallback behavior (S5), engine installation

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, pre-flight at sprint start detects a missing engine and surfaces it before the first task executes
  ← Satisfies: "Pre-flight engine check runs at sprint start, validating all engines referenced in the plan before any tasks execute"
- When this story is complete, pre-flight validates engine version against the registry's minimum_version field, not just presence
  ← Satisfies: version compatibility (adversarial review finding)
- When this story is complete, pre-flight is a no-op when the plan contains no recipe: tasks
  ← Satisfies: "must not block epics with zero recipe tasks"
- When this story is complete, pre-flight populates a missing-engines list that S5 consumes at execution time (single source of truth for engine availability)
  ← Satisfies: S4↔S5 contract (adversarial review finding)
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Pre-flight warns and populates fallback list — does not block sprint. User can choose to install engines or proceed with LLM fallback.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
